title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved Hong Kong bakery classic, featuring a signature crispy, sweet crust resembling a pineapple atop a soft, fluffy milk bread bun. Despite the name, there's no actual pineapple in these delightful treats, just pure comfort and a perfect balance of textures. Enjoy them warm for an authentic taste of Chinese dim sum culture, whether for breakfast or a satisfying snack.

instructions.title: Prepare the Bun Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine flour, 30g sugar, yeast, and salt. Add warm milk and the whole egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Turn onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate the 25g softened butter and knead for another 5 minutes until fully absorbed and the dough passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proof the Dough
instructions.text: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 45-60 minutes, or until doubled in size. While the dough is rising, prepare the pineapple topping.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, cream together the 60g softened butter and 50g sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk until well combined. Sift in the 100g flour, milk powder, and baking powder. Mix until a soft dough forms. Do not overmix. Wrap in plastic and refrigerate for 15 minutes to firm up.

instructions.title: Shape the Buns and Topping
instructions.text: Once the bun dough has doubled, punch it down gently and divide it into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. Divide the chilled pineapple topping into 8 equal portions. Flatten each topping portion into a disc, about 3-4 inches in diameter. Place a dough ball in the center of each topping disc and gently wrap the topping around the top half of the bun, leaving the bottom exposed.

instructions.title: Second Proof and Preheat Oven
instructions.text: Place the shaped buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy. Preheat your oven to 180 degrees Celsius (350 degrees Fahrenheit) during the last 15 minutes of proofing.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Just before baking, gently brush the topping of each bun with egg wash. Use a small knife to lightly score a crisscross pattern on the topping to enhance the pineapple-like appearance. Bake for 15-18 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and crispy, and the buns are cooked through. Remove from oven and let cool slightly on a wire rack before serving.

tips: For an extra fluffy bun, ensure your milk is warm but not hot (around 40-45 degrees Celsius) to activate the yeast effectively.
When making the topping, avoid overworking the dough to keep it tender and crumbly, which gives the signature crispy texture.
Pineapple Buns are best enjoyed warm, straight from the oven. If storing, reheat them briefly in a toaster oven to revive the crispy topping.
article:
The tantalizing aroma of a freshly baked bun, warm and inviting, is a quintessential experience in any Hong Kong bakery. Among the myriad of delightful pastries, one stands out as an undisputed icon: the Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao. This humble yet extraordinary creation has captured the hearts and palates of millions worldwide, its unique texture and flavor a testament to the artistry of Chinese baking. If you’ve ever dreamt of recreating that magical bakery experience in your own kitchen, you’re in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to bake your very own authentic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao, a treat that promises a soft, fluffy interior crowned with a crisp, sweet, and crumbly crust.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

Despite its name, the Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao contains no actual pineapple. The name derives purely from its distinctive topping, which, when baked, develops a golden-brown, cracked surface that strikingly resembles the rough skin of a pineapple. This signature topping is a sweet, buttery, and slightly crumbly cookie-like crust that provides a delightful contrast to the soft, airy, and mildly sweet bread underneath. Originating in Hong Kong, Bo Lo Bao quickly became a staple in cha chaan tengs (Hong Kong-style diners) and bakeries, often enjoyed for breakfast or as an afternoon tea snack. Its popularity has spread globally, making it a beloved symbol of Cantonese culinary comfort. The magic lies in the interplay of textures: the crisp, sugary exterior giving way to a cloud-like interior, making each bite an experience to savor.

## Ingredients You Need

To embark on your Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao baking adventure, gather the following ingredients. Precision here will lead to the best results.

For the Bread Dough:
*   300g bread flour (high protein flour)
*   50g granulated sugar
*   4g instant dry yeast
*   4g salt
*   1 large egg (about 50g), at room temperature
*   120ml warm milk (about 40°C / 105°F)
*   40g unsalted butter, softened

For the Pineapple Topping:
*   80g unsalted butter, softened
*   80g granulated sugar
*   1 large egg yolk (from a large egg)
*   1 teaspoon vanilla extract (optional, but recommended)
*   120g all-purpose flour
*   1 tablespoon milk powder (optional, enhances flavor)
*   1/4 teaspoon baking powder

For the Egg Wash:
*   1 egg yolk
*   1 teaspoon milk

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Baking Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a rewarding process that combines the art of bread making with a delicate topping. Follow these steps carefully for success.

1.  **Prepare the Bread Dough:** In a large mixing bowl, combine the bread flour, sugar, yeast, and salt. Mix well. Add the egg and warm milk, then stir until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a clean surface and knead for about 10-15 minutes, or until it becomes smooth and elastic. If using a stand mixer, knead with a dough hook for 8-10 minutes.
2.  **Incorporate Butter:** Add the softened butter to the dough and continue kneading until the butter is fully incorporated and the dough is very smooth and elastic, passing the windowpane test (you should be able to stretch a small piece thin enough to see light through it without tearing).
3.  **First Proofing:** Shape the dough into a ball and place it in a lightly oiled bowl. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it proof in a warm place for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.
4.  **Prepare the Pineapple Topping:** While the dough is proofing, make the topping. In a separate bowl, cream the softened butter and sugar together until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk and vanilla extract until well combined. Sift in the all-purpose flour, milk powder, and baking powder, then mix until just combined and a soft dough forms. Do not overmix. Wrap the topping dough in plastic wrap and chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
5.  **Shape the Buns:** Once the bread dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release the air. Divide the dough into 8-10 equal portions (about 60-70g each). Shape each portion into a smooth ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between each.
6.  **Shape the Topping:** Take the chilled pineapple topping dough and divide it into the same number of portions as your bread dough. Roll each portion into a ball, then flatten it into a disc (about 3-4 inches in diameter). The disc should be thin enough to cover the top of your bun.
7.  **Assemble the Pineapple Buns:** Carefully place one flattened topping disc over each bread dough ball, gently pressing it down to adhere. The topping should cover most of the top surface, but not the sides. Using a small knife or a pastry scraper, gently score a crosshatch pattern on the surface of the topping. This creates the signature "pineapple" look.
8.  **Second Proofing:** Cover the assembled buns loosely with plastic wrap and let them proof in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
9.  **Egg Wash and Bake:** Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Whisk together the egg yolk and milk for the egg wash. Gently brush the tops of the buns (only the topping, avoid the sides of the bread) with the egg wash.
10. **Bake:** Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through. The exact time may vary depending on your oven.
11. **Cool:** Transfer the baked Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect bakery-quality Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao requires a few insider tricks:

*   **Kneading is Key:** Don't skimp on kneading the bread dough. Proper gluten development is essential for a soft, airy bun. A stand mixer makes this much easier.
*   **Warmth for Proofing:** Ensure your proofing environment is warm and free of drafts. A slightly warm oven (turned off) or a proofing box works wonders.
*   **Chilled Topping:** Chilling the pineapple topping dough is crucial. It makes it easier to handle and prevents it from melting too quickly in the oven, ensuring a distinct, crumbly crust.
*   **Gentle Handling:** Handle the dough and topping gently, especially during shaping and after the second proofing, to avoid deflating the air bubbles.
*   **Scoring:** The scoring on the topping isn't just for looks; it helps the topping crack beautifully as it bakes, enhancing the "pineapple" effect.
*   **Don't Over-Egg Wash:** Apply the egg wash lightly and only to the topping. If it drips down the sides of the bun, it can hinder the rise of the bread.
*   **Monitor Oven:** Ovens vary, so keep an eye on your buns. If the topping browns too quickly, you can loosely tent them with foil.

## Serving Suggestions

A freshly baked Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a delight on its own, but there are traditional ways to elevate the experience. The most classic pairing is with a thick slice of cold butter inserted into the warm bun, creating a harmonious blend of sweet, salty, and creamy. This is often referred to as "Bo Lo Yau" (pineapple bun with butter). It’s also a perfect accompaniment to a cup of Hong Kong-style milk tea or coffee, making for a truly authentic Hong Kong breakfast or afternoon tea. For a simple snack, enjoy it warm with nothing else, letting the textures and flavors speak for themselves.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is a masterpiece, there's always room for creative twists:

*   **Fillings:** Traditionally, Bo Lo Bao is unfilled, but many bakeries now offer versions with sweet or savory fillings. Popular options include:
    *   **Custard:** A rich, creamy custard filling adds another layer of indulgence.
    *   **Red Bean Paste:** A sweet, earthy red bean paste is a common and delicious choice.
    *   **Char Siu:** For a savory twist, a barbecued pork (char siu) filling makes for a hearty meal.
*   **Flour Substitutions:** While bread flour is ideal for the bun's texture, you can experiment with a blend of all-purpose and bread flour if high-protein bread flour is hard to find. For the topping, all-purpose flour is standard, but a small amount of cake flour can make it even more tender.
*   **Milk Powder:** While optional, milk powder in the topping adds a depth of flavor and enhances the richness. If you don't have it, you can omit it, but the taste might be slightly less complex.
*   **Egg Wash Alternatives:** If you prefer a less shiny finish or have dietary restrictions, you can brush the topping with milk or a plant-based milk alternative before baking.

Baking your own Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao is more than just following a recipe; it's an immersive journey into the heart of Chinese baking. The effort invested in kneading the dough and crafting the signature topping culminates in a reward that is truly unparalleled: a warm, fragrant bun, perfectly soft on the inside and irresistibly crisp on the outside. So roll up your sleeves, gather your ingredients, and prepare to fill your home with the comforting aroma of this beloved Hong Kong classic. Enjoy the process, and savor every delicious bite of your homemade Bo Lo Bao.
